name: Rocky Mountain Development Council
description_short: Montana nonprofit providing energy assistance, weatherization, and heating support for low-income households through LIHEAP and other programs.
description_long:
Rocky Mountain Development Council is a Montana-based nonprofit organization that has served its community for 60 years, striving to improve quality of life and promote self-sufficiency for individuals and families across eight distinct programs. The organization employs over 150 staff members across more than 20 work sites throughout the region. Their primary focus in energy services is helping Montanans manage heating and weatherization costs, which are significant financial burdens in the northern climate.

Rocky's Energy Services offers three main programs: LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program) for heating cost assistance between October 1 and April 30, Energy Share for emergency energy bill payment assistance, and a Weatherization Program designed to reduce long-term heating costs through energy conservation improvements. All three programs target qualified low-income households. Their Energy Services office is located at the Neighborhood Center at 200 S Cruse Ave in Helena, Montana, operating 7:00 am to 3:00 pm. Application assistance is available for LIHEAP through April 30 annually, with dedicated staff helping households navigate the application process.

What distinguishes Rocky Mountain Development Council is its comprehensive, integrated approach to energy assistance. Rather than offering a single program, they provide multiple pathways for households to address both immediate heating emergencies and long-term energy efficiency. Their connection to broader community programs—including Head Start, AmeriCorps Seniors, Agency on Aging services, and weatherization initiatives—positions them as a true community development organization rather than a narrow assistance provider. They actively engage the community through newsletters, events, and a community needs assessment process.

Rocky Mountain Development Council is genuinely a free nonprofit resource appropriate for low-income Montana residents struggling with heating and energy costs. The main caveat is that services are geographically limited to their service area and assistance is seasonal (LIHEAP specifically runs October-April). Applicants should verify their income eligibility and residency requirements before applying.
pros: LIHEAP program provides direct heating cost assistance for qualified low-income households during winter months
Energy Share program offers emergency energy bill payment assistance beyond standard LIHEAP limits
Weatherization program provides long-term energy conservation improvements to reduce ongoing heating costs
Application assistance available—staff help households navigate the LIHEAP process rather than requiring self-service
Established 60-year history as a community-trusted nonprofit organization
Multiple energy assistance pathways allow households to access different types of support based on their situation
Located in accessible community center with extended operating hours (7am-3pm)
cons: Services appear geographically limited to Montana, likely specific service areas within the state
LIHEAP assistance is seasonal only (October 1 - April 30), leaving summer months uncovered
Website content does not specify income eligibility thresholds or maximum assistance amounts
No information provided about average wait times for weatherization work or typical assistance amounts
Energy Share is described as a separate private nonprofit organization, requiring separate application process
best_for: Low-income Montana residents struggling to pay winter heating bills
Homeowners seeking weatherization improvements to reduce long-term energy costs
Families facing energy emergencies who need immediate bill payment assistance
diagnosis: Rocky Mountain Development Council is best for low-income Montana residents needing help with heating costs or home weatherization. The primary limitation is seasonal availability (LIHEAP runs October-April only) and geographic restriction to their Montana service area; applicants should verify residency and income eligibility before contacting.
services: LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program) application and assistance for heating costs
Energy Share emergency energy bill payment assistance
Weatherization program with energy conservation home improvements
Energy Share application support and counseling
Senior Center programs and meal services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
